description: This 48KHz/24bit stereo sample pack contains 70 loops (68Mb) of improvised percussion instruments at 130BPM. Sources were placed on the studio floor and played with two regular drumsticks. A central AKG C414 in omni config through NPNG preamp was the closest mic. Two Josephson C617s through Millennia Media preamps captured the room ambience. Sources included water bottles, large vacuum cleaner pipes, wood offcuts, food containers and various other objects which were never meant to be used like this. All sources were recorded into Pro Tools through Frontier Design Group converters and large amounts of Beat Detective were employed to make something decent out of our terrible playing. Final processing was carried out in Cool Edit Pro. Recorded by Brady Leduc and myself at Pulsworks Audio Arts.

description: This is a loop created using Audacity for dare-29. http://www.freesound.org/forum/dare-the-community/35414/ First I created a click track in Audacity, at 120 BPM 4/4. The I loaded the bassdrum. I added another sound to the bassdrum to create variation in the bar and 1/2 bar hits. Followed by the hihats. - I used a pre-made hihat pattern, but I cut it up and reassembled parts of it to create my own pattern (including a reversed hit and one I stretched in Audacity using the build in Paulstrewtch). Then I loaded a clap and the snare and placed these sounds to create a pattern. I also used Audacity volume envelopt to shorten one of the snare hits to fit the mix better. I finished off by placing the cymbal and the metal rattle to add interest to the loop. No other effects were used (besides Paulstretch mentioned above) and reversing and volume envelope (if you actually consider those as envelopes). Everything was done in Audacity and the mixing was achieved simply by adjusting the volume of some of the samples, as needed. Sounds used: http://www.freesound.org/people/mishicu/sounds/195747/ http://www.freesound.org/people/OwlStorm/sounds/209015/ http://www.freesound.org/people/Sound1A/sounds/146351/ http://www.freesound.org/people/young_daddy/sounds/24787/ http://www.freesound.org/people/it_was_the_sun/sounds/209513/ http://www.freesound.org/people/Alteration/sounds/191490/ http://www.freesound.org/people/jorickhoofd/sounds/160047/

description: This is loop 34 in a series of 40 loops that belong together. They all have a deep dubspace feel at 60 bpm and belong to the "Convoloops pack 01 - back to back dubspace 60 bpm" sample pack. They all have the same name: "convoluted back to back loop 60 bpm" with a number and letter suffix (1a till 5h). Each group with the same number but with different letters are based on the same sounds and feel. The most rhythmic ones are these with suffix a till d and these with e till h are more effects. They were created using the microtonik VSTi. I took the back to back preset and convoluted it with some variations of itself. After this process I added some more convolution with another SIR, a resonator effect from MHC, and some more character with (you never guess it) the excellent Character plugin from my TC powercore firewire. All this was done within Cubase SX. After that I mastered these loops within Wavelab using several plugins: fades, equalising, multiband compressing, limiting & dither.
name: convoluted back to back loop 60 bpm 5b.wav

description: This rhythmic drone loop is one of the " Convoloops pack 03 - China Dream dronescapes 120 bpm" samplepack. These loops all belong together and are variations and alterations of each other. They all are 1 bar 4/4 long and have 120 bpm as tempo. They can be used as background loops for ambient music. Each loop has the same name with a letter an a number in the end. I took the microtonik VSTi within Cubase SX and exported the China Dream loop, which is four bars long, as 24 bit wav file. This file was then imported as impulse file within the freeware SIR convolution reverb and applied it to the original loop, all wet. So I convoluted a drumloop with itself! After that, two more reverb units were applied: another SIR (this time with an impulse file from one of the fabulous Spirit Canyon Audio CD's) and the excellent Classic Reverb from my TC Powercore Firewire (preset: Deep Space). Each of these reverbs was set all wet. When I did that, I got an 8 bar loop. This loop was then sliced up into 8 peaces of each 1 bar. So I got 8 short one bar loops: I numbered them with numbers 00 till 07. I repeated the whole process for several impulse files for the second SIR: the results are the variation a till r. There are two exceptions on the all wet rule: the two last loop sets, q and r, have their reverbs not set all wet, so these are the most rhythmic ones, since there is some of the original drumloop left. All the others are rhythmic but very vague. The most busy ones are each time numbers 03 and 04. 01 can be used as an intro and 07 is kind of the tail. All loops are normalised and mastered within Wavelab using several high quality plugins (EQ, multiband finaliser, limiter). Enjoy!
name: China Dream Convoloop a-02.wav

description: This rhythmic drone loop is one of the " Convoloops pack 03 - China Dream dronescapes 120 bpm" samplepack. These loops all belong together and are variations and alterations of each other. They all are 1 bar 4/4 long and have 120 bpm as tempo. They can be used as background loops for ambient music. Each loop has the same name with a letter an a number in the end. I took the microtonik VSTi within Cubase SX and exported the China Dream loop, which is four bars long, as 24 bit wav file. This file was then imported as impulse file within the freeware SIR convolution reverb and applied it to the original loop, all wet. So I convoluted a drumloop with itself! After that, two more reverb units were applied: another SIR (this time with an impulse file from one of the fabulous Spirit Canyon Audio CD's) and the excellent Classic Reverb from my TC Powercore Firewire (preset: Deep Space). Each of these reverbs was set all wet. When I did that, I got an 8 bar loop. This loop was then sliced up into 8 peaces of each 1 bar. So I got 8 short one bar loops: I numbered them with numbers 00 till 07. I repeated the whole process for several impulse files for the second SIR: the results are the variation a till r. There are two exceptions on the all wet rule: the two last loop sets, q and r, have their reverbs not set all wet, so these are the most rhythmic ones, since there is some of the original drumloop left. All the others are rhythmic but very vague. The most busy ones are each time numbers 03 and 04. 01 can be used as an intro and 07 is kind of the tail. All loops are normalised and mastered within Wavelab using several high quality plugins (EQ, multiband finaliser, limiter). Enjoy!
name: China Dream Convoloop b-05.wav

description: This is loop 3 in a series of 40 loops that belong together. They all have a deep dubspace feel at 60 bpm and belong to the "Convoloops pack 01 - back to back dubspace 60 bpm" sample pack. They all have the same name: "convoluted back to back loop 60 bpm" with a number and letter suffix (1a till 5h). Each group with the same number but with different letters are based on the same sounds and feel. The most rhythmic ones are these with suffix a till d and these with e till h are more effects. They were created using the microtonik VSTi. I took the back to back preset and convoluted it with some variations of itself. After this process I added some more convolution with another SIR, a resonator effect from MHC, and some more character with (you never guess it) the excellent Character plugin from my TC powercore firewire. All this was done within Cubase SX. After that I mastered these loops within Wavelab using several plugins: fades, equalising, multiband compressing, limiting & dither.
name: convoluted back to back loop 60 bpm 1c.wav

These loops all belong together and are variations and alterations of each other. They all are 1 bar 4/4 long and have 120 bpm as tempo. They can be used as background loops for ambient music. Each loop has the same name with a letter an a number in the end. I took the microtonik VSTi within Cubase SX and exported the China Dream loop, which is four bars long, as 24 bit wav file. This file was then imported as impulse file within the freeware SIR convolution reverb and applied it to the original loop, all wet. So I convoluted a drumloop with itself! After that, two more reverb units were applied: another SIR (this time with an impulse file from one of the fabulous Spirit Canyon Audio CD's) and the excellent Classic Reverb from my TC Powercore Firewire (preset: Deep Space). Each of these reverbs was set all wet. When I did that, I got an 8 bar loop. This loop was then sliced up into 8 peaces of each 1 bar. So I got 8 short one bar loops: I numbered them with numbers 00 till 07. I repeated the whole process for several impulse files for the second SIR: the results are the variation a till r. There are two exceptions on the all wet rule: the two last loop sets, q and r, have their reverbs not set all wet, so these are the most rhythmic ones, since there is some of the original drumloop left. All the others are rhythmic but very vague. The most busy ones are each time numbers 03 and 04. 01 can be used as an intro and 07 is kind of the tail. All loops are normalised and mastered within Wavelab using several high quality plugins (EQ, multiband finaliser, limiter). Enjoy!
name: China Dream Convoloop h-05.wav

This should result in a slight humanization and organic flavor.

description: Ok, I am trying to make an epic 91 bpm neo classical instrumental and needed galloping synth triplets. This is what I got. Some were made with careless mistakes like the swing function turned up on the drum machine and the tempo was set to 89 on a few of the synth loops. This should result in a slight humanization and organic flavor. Pitch and timbre are indicated in file name and tags. The loops are meant to be 3/4 time but I found that pasting them into a 120 bpm seqencer of 4/4 tempo results in perfect triplet timing... so the drums might be useless for this project. If pasting into DAW at 91 bpm, overlap or cut off the silence or delay trail as applicable.

description: This rhythmic drone loop is one of the " Convoloops pack 03 - China Dream dronescapes 120 bpm" samplepack. These loops all belong together and are variations and alterations of each other. They all are 1 bar 4/4 long and have 120 bpm as tempo. They can be used as background loops for ambient music. Each loop has the same name with a letter an a number in the end. I took the microtonik VSTi within Cubase SX and exported the China Dream loop, which is four bars long, as 24 bit wav file. This file was then imported as impulse file within the freeware SIR convolution reverb and applied it to the original loop, all wet. So I convoluted a drumloop with itself! After that, two more reverb units were applied: another SIR (this time with an impulse file from one of the fabulous Spirit Canyon Audio CD's) and the excellent Classic Reverb from my TC Powercore Firewire (preset: Deep Space). Each of these reverbs was set all wet. When I did that, I got an 8 bar loop. This loop was then sliced up into 8 peaces of each 1 bar. So I got 8 short one bar loops: I numbered them with numbers 00 till 07. I repeated the whole process for several impulse files for the second SIR: the results are the variation a till r. There are two exceptions on the all wet rule: the two last loop sets, q and r, have their reverbs not set all wet, so these are the most rhythmic ones, since there is some of the original drumloop left. All the others are rhythmic but very vague. The most busy ones are each time numbers 03 and 04. 01 can be used as an intro and 07 is kind of the tail. All loops are normalised and mastered within Wavelab using several high quality plugins (EQ, multiband finaliser, limiter). Enjoy!
name: China Dream Convoloop p-07.wav

description: This rhythmic drone loop is one of the " Convoloops pack 03 - China Dream dronescapes 120 bpm" samplepack. These loops all belong together and are variations and alterations of each other. They all are 1 bar 4/4 long and have 120 bpm as tempo. They can be used as background loops for ambient music. Each loop has the same name with a letter an a number in the end. I took the microtonik VSTi within Cubase SX and exported the China Dream loop, which is four bars long, as 24 bit wav file. This file was then imported as impulse file within the freeware SIR convolution reverb and applied it to the original loop, all wet. So I convoluted a drumloop with itself! After that, two more reverb units were applied: another SIR (this time with an impulse file from one of the fabulous Spirit Canyon Audio CD's) and the excellent Classic Reverb from my TC Powercore Firewire (preset: Deep Space). Each of these reverbs was set all wet. When I did that, I got an 8 bar loop. This loop was then sliced up into 8 peaces of each 1 bar. So I got 8 short one bar loops: I numbered them with numbers 00 till 07. I repeated the whole process for several impulse files for the second SIR: the results are the variation a till r. There are two exceptions on the all wet rule: the two last loop sets, q and r, have their reverbs not set all wet, so these are the most rhythmic ones, since there is some of the original drumloop left. All the others are rhythmic but very vague. The most busy ones are each time numbers 03 and 04. 01 can be used as an intro and 07 is kind of the tail. All loops are normalised and mastered within Wavelab using several high quality plugins (EQ, multiband finaliser, limiter). Enjoy!
name: China Dream Convoloop r-03.wav

description: This is loop 50 in a series of 135 loops that belong together. They all have a deep dubspace feel in 1 bar 4/4 at 60 bpm and belong to the "Convoloops pack 02 - bare bone dubspace 60 bpm" sample pack. They all have the same name: "convoluted bare bone loop 60 bpm" with three numbers as suffix. The first of the three numbers indicates a group of samples with a similar sound and feel. The most rhythmic ones are these with 1 till 4 as last number in the suffix and these with 5 till 8 are more effects. Finally number 9 as the last number in the suffix is the start of the delay and/or reverb tail of the previous loop. The second number separates variations in pitch of the initial loop before any processing is applied. Of these variations number 5 is more granular & experimental. All loops were created using the microtonik VSTi. I took the bare bones preset and convoluted it with some variations of itself. After this process I added some more convolution with another SIR, some diffusion, delay & chorus with the Fusion Reflector ensemble from Native Instruments Reaktor, and some more character with (you never guess it) the excellent Character plugin from my TC powercore firewire. All this was done within Cubase SX. After that I mastered these loops within Wavelab using several plugins: fades, equalising, multiband compressing, limiting & dither.

description: This 48KHz/24bit stereo sample pack contains 70 loops (68Mb) of improvised percussion instruments at 130BPM. Sources were placed on the studio floor and played with two regular drumsticks. A central AKG C414 in omni config through NPNG preamp was the closest mic. Two Josephson C617s through Millennia Media preamps captured the room ambience. Sources included water bottles, large vacuum cleaner pipes, wood offcuts, food containers and various other objects which were never meant to be used like this. All sources were recorded into Pro Tools through Frontier Design Group converters and large amounts of Beat Detective were employed to make something decent out of our terrible playing. Final processing was carried out in Cool Edit Pro. Recorded by Brady Leduc and myself at Pulsworks Audio Arts.

description: This is loop 104 in a series of 135 loops that belong together. They all have a deep dubspace feel in 1 bar 4/4 at 60 bpm and belong to the "Convoloops pack 02 - bare bone dubspace 60 bpm" sample pack. They all have the same name: "convoluted bare bone loop 60 bpm" with three numbers as suffix. The first of the three numbers indicates a group of samples with a similar sound and feel. The most rhythmic ones are these with 1 till 4 as last number in the suffix and these with 5 till 8 are more effects. Finally number 9 as the last number in the suffix is the start of the delay and/or reverb tail of the previous loop. The second number separates variations in pitch of the initial loop before any processing is applied. Of these variations number 5 is more granular & experimental. All loops were created using the microtonik VSTi. I took the bare bones preset and convoluted it with some variations of itself. After this process I added some more convolution with another SIR, some diffusion, delay & chorus with the Fusion Reflector ensemble from Native Instruments Reaktor, and some more character with (you never guess it) the excellent Character plugin from my TC powercore firewire. All this was done within Cubase SX. After that I mastered these loops within Wavelab using several plugins: fades, equalising, multiband compressing, limiting & dither.
name: convoluted bare bone loop 60 bpm 225.wav

The most rhythmic ones are these with 1 till 4 as last number in the suffix and these with 5 till 8 are more effects. Finally number 9 as the last number in the suffix is the start of the delay and/or reverb tail of the previous loop. The second number separates variations in pitch of the initial loop before any processing is applied. Of these variations number 5 is more granular & experimental. All loops were created using the microtonik VSTi. I took the bare bones preset and convoluted it with some variations of itself. After this process I added some more convolution with another SIR, some diffusion, delay & chorus with the Fusion Reflector ensemble from Native Instruments Reaktor, and some more character with (you never guess it) the excellent Character plugin from my TC powercore firewire. All this was done within Cubase SX. After that I mastered these loops within Wavelab using several plugins: fades, equalising, multiband compressing, limiting & dither.
name: convoluted bare bone loop 60 bpm 258.wav

description: This rhythmic drone loop is one of the " Convoloops pack 03 - China Dream dronescapes 120 bpm" samplepack. These loops all belong together and are variations and alterations of each other. They all are 1 bar 4/4 long and have 120 bpm as tempo. They can be used as background loops for ambient music. Each loop has the same name with a letter an a number in the end. I took the microtonik VSTi within Cubase SX and exported the China Dream loop, which is four bars long, as 24 bit wav file. This file was then imported as impulse file within the freeware SIR convolution reverb and applied it to the original loop, all wet. So I convoluted a drumloop with itself! After that, two more reverb units were applied: another SIR (this time with an impulse file from one of the fabulous Spirit Canyon Audio CD's) and the excellent Classic Reverb from my TC Powercore Firewire (preset: Deep Space). Each of these reverbs was set all wet. When I did that, I got an 8 bar loop. This loop was then sliced up into 8 peaces of each 1 bar. So I got 8 short one bar loops: I numbered them with numbers 00 till 07. I repeated the whole process for several impulse files for the second SIR: the results are the variation a till r. There are two exceptions on the all wet rule: the two last loop sets, q and r, have their reverbs not set all wet, so these are the most rhythmic ones, since there is some of the original drumloop left. All the others are rhythmic but very vague. The most busy ones are each time numbers 03 and 04. 01 can be used as an intro and 07 is kind of the tail. All loops are normalised and mastered within Wavelab using several high quality plugins (EQ, multiband finaliser, limiter). Enjoy!
name: China Dream Convoloop m-06.wav

description: This 48KHz/24bit stereo sample pack contains 70 loops (68Mb) of improvised percussion instruments at 130BPM. Sources were placed on the studio floor and played with two regular drumsticks. A central AKG C414 in omni config through NPNG preamp was the closest mic. Two Josephson C617s through Millennia Media preamps captured the room ambience. Sources included water bottles, large vacuum cleaner pipes, wood offcuts, food containers and various other objects which were never meant to be used like this. All sources were recorded into Pro Tools through Frontier Design Group converters and large amounts of Beat Detective were employed to make something decent out of our terrible playing. Final processing was carried out in Cool Edit Pro. Recorded by Brady Leduc and myself at Pulsworks Audio Arts.
name: IMPROV PERCS 072 1 BAR 130 BPM.wav

description: This is loop 1 in a series of 40 loops that belong together. They all have a deep dubspace feel at 60 bpm and belong to the "Convoloops pack 01 - back to back dubspace 60 bpm" sample pack. They all have the same name: "convoluted back to back loop 60 bpm" with a number and letter suffix (1a till 5h). Each group with the same number but with different letters are based on the same sounds and feel. The most rhythmic ones are these with suffix a till d and these with e till h are more effects. They were created using the microtonik VSTi. I took the back to back preset and convoluted it with some variations of itself. After this process I added some more convolution with another SIR, a resonator effect from MHC, and some more character with (you never guess it) the excellent Character plugin from my TC powercore firewire. All this was done within Cubase SX. After that I mastered these loops within Wavelab using several plugins: fades, equalising, multiband compressing, limiting & dither.
name: convoluted back to back loop 60 bpm 1a.wav

description: This is loop 1 in a series of 135 loops that belong together. They all have a deep dubspace feel in 1 bar 4/4 at 60 bpm and belong to the "Convoloops pack 02 - bare bone dubspace 60 bpm" sample pack. They all have the same name: "convoluted bare bone loop 60 bpm" with three numbers as suffix. The first of the three numbers indicates a group of samples with a similar sound and feel. The most rhythmic ones are these with 1 till 4 as last number in the suffix and these with 5 till 8 are more effects. Finally number 9 as the last number in the suffix is the start of the delay and/or reverb tail of the previous loop. The second number separates variations in pitch of the initial loop before any processing is applied. Of these variations number 5 is more granular & experimental. All loops were created using the microtonik VSTi. I took the bare bones preset and convoluted it with some variations of itself. After this process I added some more convolution with another SIR, some diffusion, delay & chorus with the Fusion Reflector ensemble from Native Instruments Reaktor, and some more character with (you never guess it) the excellent Character plugin from my TC powercore firewire. All this was done within Cubase SX. After that I mastered these loops within Wavelab using several plugins: fades, equalising, multiband compressing, limiting & dither.
name: convoluted bare bone loop 60 bpm 011.wav

They all have a deep dubspace feel in 1 bar 4/4 at 60 bpm and belong to the "Convoloops pack 02 - bare bone dubspace 60 bpm" sample pack. They all have the same name: "convoluted bare bone loop 60 bpm" with three numbers as suffix. The first of the three numbers indicates a group of samples with a similar sound and feel. The most rhythmic ones are these with 1 till 4 as last number in the suffix and these with 5 till 8 are more effects. Finally number 9 as the last number in the suffix is the start of the delay and/or reverb tail of the previous loop. The second number separates variations in pitch of the initial loop before any processing is applied. Of these variations number 5 is more granular & experimental. All loops were created using the microtonik VSTi. I took the bare bones preset and convoluted it with some variations of itself. After this process I added some more convolution with another SIR, some diffusion, delay & chorus with the Fusion Reflector ensemble from Native Instruments Reaktor, and some more character with (you never guess it) the excellent Character plugin from my TC powercore firewire. All this was done within Cubase SX. After that I mastered these loops within Wavelab using several plugins: fades, equalising, multiband compressing, limiting & dither.
name: convoluted bare bone loop 60 bpm 223.wav

They all have a deep dubspace feel in 1 bar 4/4 at 60 bpm and belong to the "Convoloops pack 02 - bare bone dubspace 60 bpm" sample pack. They all have the same name: "convoluted bare bone loop 60 bpm" with three numbers as suffix. The first of the three numbers indicates a group of samples with a similar sound and feel. The most rhythmic ones are these with 1 till 4 as last number in the suffix and these with 5 till 8 are more effects. Finally number 9 as the last number in the suffix is the start of the delay and/or reverb tail of the previous loop. The second number separates variations in pitch of the initial loop before any processing is applied. Of these variations number 5 is more granular & experimental. All loops were created using the microtonik VSTi. I took the bare bones preset and convoluted it with some variations of itself. After this process I added some more convolution with another SIR, some diffusion, delay & chorus with the Fusion Reflector ensemble from Native Instruments Reaktor, and some more character with (you never guess it) the excellent Character plugin from my TC powercore firewire. All this was done within Cubase SX. After that I mastered these loops within Wavelab using several plugins: fades, equalising, multiband compressing, limiting & dither.
name: convoluted bare bone loop 60 bpm 254.wav

description: This 48KHz/24bit stereo sample pack contains 70 loops (68Mb) of improvised percussion instruments at 130BPM. Sources were placed on the studio floor and played with two regular drumsticks. A central AKG C414 in omni config through NPNG preamp was the closest mic. Two Josephson C617s through Millennia Media preamps captured the room ambience. Sources included water bottles, large vacuum cleaner pipes, wood offcuts, food containers and various other objects which were never meant to be used like this. All sources were recorded into Pro Tools through Frontier Design Group converters and large amounts of Beat Detective were employed to make something decent out of our terrible playing. Final processing was carried out in Cool Edit Pro. Recorded by Brady Leduc and myself at Pulsworks Audio Arts.
name: IMPROV PERCS 076 1 BAR 130 BPM.wav

description: This sample was created solely from instruments downloaded on freesound.org. Having experimented more, I created another break with LFO band frequencies adjusted to create a white noise type break. I then played them simultaneously and mixed one into the other to create the effect of moving the EQ in real time. To fully appreciate this, you need to download and play it seemlessly looped. This is recorded at 90 bpm for the continuum 4 project. You can visit the thread here: http://www.freesound.org/forum/dare-the-community/32704/ Here are the sounds used in the break: snare: http://www.freesound.org/people/ltibbits/sounds/16306/ kick boom: http://www.freesound.org/people/jobro/sounds/33523/ short kick: http://www.freesound.org/people/crescendo/sounds/16709/ closed hat: http://www.freesound.org/people/atomwrath/sounds/11394/ rim: http://www.freesound.org/people/opm/sounds/2095/ open hat: http://www.freesound.org/people/TicTacShutUp/sounds/423/ static: http://www.freesound.org/people/hello_flowers/sounds/30974/ rev crash: http://www.freesound.org/people/Halleck/sounds/30938/

description: This 48KHz/24bit stereo sample pack contains 70 loops (68Mb) of improvised percussion instruments at 130BPM. Sources were placed on the studio floor and played with two regular drumsticks. A central AKG C414 in omni config through NPNG preamp was the closest mic. Two Josephson C617s through Millennia Media preamps captured the room ambience. Sources included water bottles, large vacuum cleaner pipes, wood offcuts, food containers and various other objects which were never meant to be used like this. All sources were recorded into Pro Tools through Frontier Design Group converters and large amounts of Beat Detective were employed to make something decent out of our terrible playing. Final processing was carried out in Cool Edit Pro. Recorded by Brady Leduc and myself at Pulsworks Audio Arts.

description: This is loop 32 in a series of 135 loops that belong together. They all have a deep dubspace feel in 1 bar 4/4 at 60 bpm and belong to the "Convoloops pack 02 - bare bone dubspace 60 bpm" sample pack. They all have the same name: "convoluted bare bone loop 60 bpm" with three numbers as suffix. The first of the three numbers indicates a group of samples with a similar sound and feel. The most rhythmic ones are these with 1 till 4 as last number in the suffix and these with 5 till 8 are more effects. Finally number 9 as the last number in the suffix is the start of the delay and/or reverb tail of the previous loop. The second number separates variations in pitch of the initial loop before any processing is applied. Of these variations number 5 is more granular & experimental. All loops were created using the microtonik VSTi. I took the bare bones preset and convoluted it with some variations of itself. After this process I added some more convolution with another SIR, some diffusion, delay & chorus with the Fusion Reflector ensemble from Native Instruments Reaktor, and some more character with (you never guess it) the excellent Character plugin from my TC powercore firewire. All this was done within Cubase SX. After that I mastered these loops within Wavelab using several plugins: fades, equalising, multiband compressing, limiting & dither.
name: convoluted bare bone loop 60 bpm 045.wav

description: This rhythmic drone loop is one of the " Convoloops pack 03 - China Dream dronescapes 120 bpm" samplepack. These loops all belong together and are variations and alterations of each other. They all are 1 bar 4/4 long and have 120 bpm as tempo. They can be used as background loops for ambient music. Each loop has the same name with a letter an a number in the end. I took the microtonik VSTi within Cubase SX and exported the China Dream loop, which is four bars long, as 24 bit wav file. This file was then imported as impulse file within the freeware SIR convolution reverb and applied it to the original loop, all wet. So I convoluted a drumloop with itself! After that, two more reverb units were applied: another SIR (this time with an impulse file from one of the fabulous Spirit Canyon Audio CD's) and the excellent Classic Reverb from my TC Powercore Firewire (preset: Deep Space). Each of these reverbs was set all wet. When I did that, I got an 8 bar loop. This loop was then sliced up into 8 peaces of each 1 bar. So I got 8 short one bar loops: I numbered them with numbers 00 till 07. I repeated the whole process for several impulse files for the second SIR: the results are the variation a till r. There are two exceptions on the all wet rule: the two last loop sets, q and r, have their reverbs not set all wet, so these are the most rhythmic ones, since there is some of the original drumloop left. All the others are rhythmic but very vague. The most busy ones are each time numbers 03 and 04. 01 can be used as an intro and 07 is kind of the tail. All loops are normalised and mastered within Wavelab using several high quality plugins (EQ, multiband finaliser, limiter). Enjoy!
name: China Dream Convoloop a-00.wav

These loops all belong together and are variations and alterations of each other. They all are 1 bar 4/4 long and have 120 bpm as tempo. They can be used as background loops for ambient music. Each loop has the same name with a letter an a number in the end. I took the microtonik VSTi within Cubase SX and exported the China Dream loop, which is four bars long, as 24 bit wav file. This file was then imported as impulse file within the freeware SIR convolution reverb and applied it to the original loop, all wet. So I convoluted a drumloop with itself! After that, two more reverb units were applied: another SIR (this time with an impulse file from one of the fabulous Spirit Canyon Audio CD's) and the excellent Classic Reverb from my TC Powercore Firewire (preset: Deep Space). Each of these reverbs was set all wet. When I did that, I got an 8 bar loop. This loop was then sliced up into 8 peaces of each 1 bar. So I got 8 short one bar loops: I numbered them with numbers 00 till 07. I repeated the whole process for several impulse files for the second SIR: the results are the variation a till r. There are two exceptions on the all wet rule: the two last loop sets, q and r, have their reverbs not set all wet, so these are the most rhythmic ones, since there is some of the original drumloop left. All the others are rhythmic but very vague. The most busy ones are each time numbers 03 and 04. 01 can be used as an intro and 07 is kind of the tail. All loops are normalised and mastered within Wavelab using several high quality plugins (EQ, multiband finaliser, limiter). Enjoy!

Each loop has the same name with a letter an a number in the end. I took the microtonik VSTi within Cubase SX and exported the China Dream loop, which is four bars long, as 24 bit wav file. This file was then imported as impulse file within the freeware SIR convolution reverb and applied it to the original loop, all wet. So I convoluted a drumloop with itself! After that, two more reverb units were applied: another SIR (this time with an impulse file from one of the fabulous Spirit Canyon Audio CD's) and the excellent Classic Reverb from my TC Powercore Firewire (preset: Deep Space). Each of these reverbs was set all wet. When I did that, I got an 8 bar loop. This loop was then sliced up into 8 peaces of each 1 bar. So I got 8 short one bar loops: I numbered them with numbers 00 till 07. I repeated the whole process for several impulse files for the second SIR: the results are the variation a till r. There are two exceptions on the all wet rule: the two last loop sets, q and r, have their reverbs not set all wet, so these are the most rhythmic ones, since there is some of the original drumloop left. All the others are rhythmic but very vague. The most busy ones are each time numbers 03 and 04. 01 can be used as an intro and 07 is kind of the tail. All loops are normalised and mastered within Wavelab using several high quality plugins (EQ, multiband finaliser, limiter). Enjoy!
name: China Dream Convoloop j-05.wav

After that, two more reverb units were applied: another SIR (this time with an impulse file from one of the fabulous Spirit Canyon Audio CD's) and the excellent Classic Reverb from my TC Powercore Firewire (preset: Deep Space). Each of these reverbs was set all wet. When I did that, I got an 8 bar loop. This loop was then sliced up into 8 peaces of each 1 bar. So I got 8 short one bar loops: I numbered them with numbers 00 till 07. I repeated the whole process for several impulse files for the second SIR: the results are the variation a till r. There are two exceptions on the all wet rule: the two last loop sets, q and r, have their reverbs not set all wet, so these are the most rhythmic ones, since there is some of the original drumloop left. All the others are rhythmic but very vague. The most busy ones are each time numbers 03 and 04. 01 can be used as an intro and 07 is kind of the tail. All loops are normalised and mastered within Wavelab using several high quality plugins (EQ, multiband finaliser, limiter). Enjoy!

description: This is loop 52 in a series of 135 loops that belong together. They all have a deep dubspace feel in 1 bar 4/4 at 60 bpm and belong to the "Convoloops pack 02 - bare bone dubspace 60 bpm" sample pack. They all have the same name: "convoluted bare bone loop 60 bpm" with three numbers as suffix. The first of the three numbers indicates a group of samples with a similar sound and feel. The most rhythmic ones are these with 1 till 4 as last number in the suffix and these with 5 till 8 are more effects. Finally number 9 as the last number in the suffix is the start of the delay and/or reverb tail of the previous loop. The second number separates variations in pitch of the initial loop before any processing is applied. Of these variations number 5 is more granular & experimental. All loops were created using the microtonik VSTi. I took the bare bones preset and convoluted it with some variations of itself. After this process I added some more convolution with another SIR, some diffusion, delay & chorus with the Fusion Reflector ensemble from Native Instruments Reaktor, and some more character with (you never guess it) the excellent Character plugin from my TC powercore firewire. All this was done within Cubase SX. After that I mastered these loops within Wavelab using several plugins: fades, equalising, multiband compressing, limiting & dither.

description: This is loop 65 in a series of 135 loops that belong together. They all have a deep dubspace feel in 1 bar 4/4 at 60 bpm and belong to the "Convoloops pack 02 - bare bone dubspace 60 bpm" sample pack. They all have the same name: "convoluted bare bone loop 60 bpm" with three numbers as suffix. The first of the three numbers indicates a group of samples with a similar sound and feel. The most rhythmic ones are these with 1 till 4 as last number in the suffix and these with 5 till 8 are more effects. Finally number 9 as the last number in the suffix is the start of the delay and/or reverb tail of the previous loop. The second number separates variations in pitch of the initial loop before any processing is applied. Of these variations number 5 is more granular & experimental. All loops were created using the microtonik VSTi. I took the bare bones preset and convoluted it with some variations of itself. After this process I added some more convolution with another SIR, some diffusion, delay & chorus with the Fusion Reflector ensemble from Native Instruments Reaktor, and some more character with (you never guess it) the excellent Character plugin from my TC powercore firewire. All this was done within Cubase SX. After that I mastered these loops within Wavelab using several plugins: fades, equalising, multiband compressing, limiting & dither.
name: convoluted bare bone loop 60 bpm 132.wav

description: This is a 130 BPM 6 bar at 4/4 loop from my Q Rack hardware synth. It is part of the "Q multi 005: 130 BPM arpeggiated loop" sample pack. The sound is on the key in the name of the file. I created several variations (1 till 6, to be found in the filename) with various settings for filter type, cutoff and resonance and I played also with the filter & amplitude envelopes. Created with Waldorf Q Rack synth, digitally recorded through my Spirit 328 Digital Console in Cubase 4 as 32 bit wave files. Only normalization, fading, dithering and conversion to flac format were applied after recording.

description: This is a 130 BPM 6 bar at 4/4 loop from my Q Rack hardware synth. It is part of the "Q multi 005: 130 BPM arpeggiated loop" sample pack. The sound is on the key in the name of the file. I created several variations (1 till 6, to be found in the filename) with various settings for filter type, cutoff and resonance and I played also with the filter & amplitude envelopes. Created with Waldorf Q Rack synth, digitally recorded through my Spirit 328 Digital Console in Cubase 4 as 32 bit wave files. Only normalization, fading, dithering and conversion to flac format were applied after recording.
name: _130 BPM arpeggiated loop - E3 - variation 6.flac

description: This is a variation of 20140306_attackloop_120BPM_4/4_Acoustic_Kit_Distorted_loop1 and it is a loop created with the Waldorf Attack VST Drum Synth. The kit used was Acoustic Kit and the loop was created using Cubase 7.5. The following plugins were used to process the signal: AnarchRhythms, StepFilter (2 times used), Guitar Rig 5, Amp Simulater and iZotome Ozone 5. Different variations have different filter settings in the Step Filter. 16 variations are labelled form a till p. Everything is at 120 bpm and measure 4/4. Enjoy!

The kit used was 23rd century Kit and the loop was created using Cubase 7.5. The following plugins were used to process the signal: AnarchRhythms, StepFilter, Guitar Rig 5 and iZotome Ozone 5. The different variants gradually change to more an more deep frequencies. 8 variations are labelled form a till h. Everything is at 120 bpm and measure 4/4. Enjoy!
